pattern: With so few numbers allowed, there's no need to be clever about which pairs qualify -- just check every pair against the strong-pair rule directly and keep the best xor you find.
hints: The array is tiny, so you can afford to compare every pair of numbers (including a number with itself) against the strong-pair condition directly.
The condition |x - y| <= min(x, y) is the same as saying the larger of the two is at most twice the smaller.
Among only the pairs that qualify, keep a running maximum of their bitwise xor.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two, only self-pairs qualify
single element, only pairs with itself
two equal elements, xor with itself is zero
one value more than double another, disqualifying the pair

stress.description: random small arrays covering the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
